dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Not applicable — this is a library with no authentication or authorization code. Score reflects absence of this concern.

findings.summary: Repeated consecutive import_optional_dependency calls in multiple files
evidence: _core.py lines 107-112: import_optional_dependency called twice consecutively
transactions.py lines 136-144: three consecutive import_optional_dependency calls
findings.suggestion: Consolidate consecutive import_optional_dependency calls into single blocks.

findings.summary: Identical is_empty check pattern duplicated across methods in model.py
evidence: Lines 57-72 and lines 177-189 contain identical is_empty checking logic
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared _is_dataframe_empty(df) utility function.

findings.summary: Module-level time import shadowed by repeated conditional imports in _core.py
evidence: time imported at line 4 but re-imported inside verbose blocks at lines 47, 53, 60, 78, 87, 94, 171
findings.suggestion: Remove redundant import time statements inside verbose blocks.

findings.summary: col_names computed twice in dispatch() for CSR path
evidence: Lines 223 and 229 both compute: col_names = column_names or [str(i) for i in range(n_cols)]
findings.suggestion: Remove the duplicate col_names assignment at line 229.

findings.summary: tuple listed twice in isinstance checks in _convert_to_orig_type
evidence: Line 538 and 552: isinstance(x, (tuple, set, tuple)) — tuple appears twice
findings.suggestion: Fix isinstance checks to use (tuple, set) without the duplicate tuple entry.

findings.summary: _auto_detect_cuda() executes at import time causing side effects
evidence: __init__.py line 72: _auto_detect_cuda() called at module import time
findings.suggestion: Defer CUDA detection to first use or make it opt-in.

findings.summary: DataFrame type detection relies on string comparison of class names
evidence: _core.py line 199: type detection via string matching __name__ and __module__
findings.suggestion: Centralize type detection into a helper function.

findings.summary: Some files import from rusket._dependencies with full path, others use relative imports
evidence: _core.py line 107 uses absolute 'from rusket._dependencies', line 8 uses relative 'from .'
findings.suggestion: Standardize on relative imports throughout the rusket package.

findings.summary: 60 files in flat package structure without sub-packages
evidence: All 60 Python files in rusket/ root without grouping into sub-packages
findings.suggestion: Consider grouping related modules into sub-packages.

findings.summary: RuleMinerMixin.recommend_items() is deprecated but still in the public API
evidence: Lines 222-231: recommend_items warns about deprecation but is still present
findings.suggestion: Add removal version to deprecation warning message.

findings.summary: Benchmark tests colocated with unit tests in tests/ directory
evidence: 4 benchmark test files in tests/ alongside unit tests without marker separation
findings.suggestion: Tag benchmark tests with pytest markers for easy exclusion from CI.

findings.summary: Bare except/pass block swallows all errors silently
evidence: Lines 69-72: except Exception: pass — silently swallows errors
findings.suggestion: Catch a more specific exception type or log the error.

findings.summary: Internal helpers use generic names that don't communicate domain intent
evidence: _run_dense, _run_sparse, _build_result are generic names that don't indicate they're for frequent pattern mining
findings.suggestion: Rename to _mine_dense, _mine_sparse, _build_itemset_result to communicate mining purpose.

findings.summary: mine_grouped has three separate code paths (Spark/Polars/Pandas) with duplicated logic
evidence: mine_grouped method spans ~100 lines with 3 near-identical processing paths for different DataFrame types
findings.suggestion: Extract common mining loop logic and parameterize the DataFrame-specific parts.

findings.summary: Excessive use of Any type annotations where more specific types could be inferred
evidence: model.py: kwargs stored as dict with Any values, _user_factors/_item_factors typed as Any instead of np.ndarray
findings.suggestion: Use np.ndarray or NDArray[np.float32] for factor arrays. Use TypeVar or Protocol for kwargs types.

findings.summary: predict() depends on implicit _result attribute being set by fit()
evidence: Lines 665-667: predict() checks hasattr(self, '_result') which is implicitly set by fit()
findings.suggestion: Initialize _result = None in __init__ and check for None explicitly.

findings.summary: model.py imports from association_rules, spark, mine creating hub module dependency
evidence: model.py deferred-imports from association_rules (line 48-49), spark (line 135), mine (line 718), _dependencies, _compat
findings.suggestion: Inject dependencies or use protocol-based abstractions to reduce model.py's coupling surface.

findings.summary: scipy is used without being listed as explicitly required in several code paths
evidence: transactions.py line 377, 452: from scipy import sparse used directly without import_optional_dependency guard
findings.suggestion: Either document scipy as a hard dependency or wrap it with import_optional_dependency like other optional deps.

findings.summary: model.py is 1131 lines containing 4 major classes and utility functions
evidence: model.py contains RuleMinerMixin, BaseModel, Miner, ImplicitRecommender, load_model, SequentialRecommender — 1131 lines
findings.suggestion: Split model.py into separate files: base.py (BaseModel), miner.py (Miner, RuleMinerMixin), recommender.py (ImplicitRecommender, SequentialRecommender).

findings.summary: DataFrame type coercion logic is scattered across multiple modules
evidence: Type detection and coercion code appears in _core.py dispatch(), model.py _convert_to_orig_type(), model.py from_transactions(), transactions.py from_transactions()
findings.suggestion: Centralize all type detection and coercion in _compat.py with a clear API.

findings.summary: batch_recommend format parameter accepts string instead of enum/literal
evidence: als.py line 383: format: str = 'polars' accepts any string, only validated at runtime
findings.suggestion: Use Literal['pandas', 'polars', 'spark'] for the format parameter type.

findings.summary: _convert_to_orig_type pattern duplicates type coercion across model base and from_transactions
evidence: model.py _convert_to_orig_type and transactions.py from_transactions both implement parallel type-routing logic for Pandas/Polars/Spark/Arrow
findings.suggestion: Unify the output-type-routing pattern into a shared utility in _compat.py.
